name: Advance America
description_short: At 518 N Montebello Blvd in Montebello, CA 90640, Advance America can be reached at +1 323-724-2966.
description_long:
Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Advance America grew into one of the largest payday lenders in the United States by physical store count. In 2012, Mexican conglomerate Grupo Elektra — controlled by billionaire Ricardo Salinas Pliego — acquired the company for approximately $780 million. A founding member of the Community Financial Services Association of America (CFSA), the payday lending industry's primary trade group, Advance America operates 750–1,500+ storefront locations across roughly 23–28 states, alongside a full online lending platform.

Advance America offers four core products. Payday loans range from $100 to $2,000 with 7–31 day repayment terms — typical APRs run 304% to 688% depending on state (e.g., ~304% APR on a $300 Florida loan, ~460% APR equivalent in California). Installment loans carry 3–12 month terms at approximately 207%–348% APR in available states. Title loans, secured by vehicle title, are available in select states at variable rates. Revolving lines of credit up to approximately $4,000 are offered in select markets. Money services including money orders and transfers round out the product set. No hard credit pull is used — approval is based on income and employment verification, making these loans accessible to borrowers with poor or no credit history. Same-day or next-business-day funding is available both online and in-store.

Advance America's competitive position rests on breadth and accessibility. With nearly three decades of operating history, a large retail footprint offering face-to-face service, and a digital portal at online.advanceamerica.net supporting real-time account management, they deliver a more integrated experience than many single-channel competitors. An A+ BBB rating reflects a low volume of unresolved consumer complaints relative to their transaction scale. Their CFSA membership subjects them to a voluntary code of conduct covering rollovers and payment plans.

The critical limitation is cost. APRs of 304%–688% on payday loans place Advance America among the most expensive legal lenders in U.S. consumer finance. The CFPB has repeatedly scrutinized the payday lending model for debt-trap dynamics — short repayment windows can push financially stressed borrowers into serial rollovers. Product availability varies sharply by state due to differing regulations (Ohio caps installment APRs at 28%; California caps loan size at $255). Advance America is a fully licensed, regulated lender operating within state law, but it is most appropriate as a genuine last resort after exhausting lower-cost options such as credit union PAL programs, CDFI loans, or employer advances.

pricing.guarantee_details: No general money-back guarantee. Some states mandate partial fee refunds for early repayment under state consumer lending law — this is not a company-wide policy.

tiers.name: Payday Loan
features: Loan amounts: $100–$2,000 (state limits apply; CA max $255)
Typical APR: ~304%–688% depending on state and amount
No hard credit check — income/employment verified

tiers.name: Title Loan
features: Loan amount based on vehicle value
Vehicle must be owned outright or nearly paid off

tiers.name: Line of Credit
features: Revolving credit line up to approximately $4,000
Draw funds as needed up to credit limit
Interest charged only on outstanding balance

pros: Founded in 1997 — nearly 30 years of operating history as a licensed consumer lender
750–1,500+ physical storefronts across ~23–28 states, one of the largest retail footprints in U.S. payday lending
No hard credit check — approval based on income and employment, accessible to borrowers with poor or no credit
Same-day or next-business-day funding available both in-store and online
A+ BBB rating reflecting relatively low unresolved complaint rate given transaction volume
Founding CFSA member, subject to voluntary code of conduct on rollover limits and extended payment plans
Online portal supports real-time account tracking, document upload, and loan management
cons: Payday loan APRs of approximately 304%–688% are among the highest in legal U.S. consumer lending
Installment loan APRs of 207%–348% remain extremely high compared to credit union or bank alternatives
Short payday loan terms (7–31 days) create debt-trap risk — CFPB has scrutinized this model for serial rollover patterns
Product availability varies significantly by state — some products unavailable in many markets due to state regulations
Owned since 2012 by Grupo Elektra, a Mexican conglomerate — not a U.S.-owned independent lender
best_for: Consumers who need $100–$2,000 before their next paycheck and have exhausted lower-cost options
Borrowers with poor, thin, or no credit history who cannot qualify for traditional bank or credit union loans
Individuals who prefer in-person service and live near an Advance America storefront
People in states where Advance America operates and who need same-day or next-day cash access
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for financially stressed borrowers who need small-dollar emergency cash quickly and cannot access traditional credit — payday loans, installment loans, and title loans are available with no hard credit check and fast funding. The primary caveat is cost: APRs of 304%–688% on payday products make this one of the most expensive forms of legal credit in the U.S., and borrowers who cannot repay on the original due date risk an expensive rollover cycle.
services: Installment Loans (3–12 month terms, select states)
Lines of Credit (revolving, up to ~$4,000, select states)
Online Account Management and Document Upload

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
